name: navigation-behavior
description: Canonical source of truth for default page navigation behavior. Other files should reference this spec instead of repeating CTA and auto-advance rules.

rules.description: Entry portal pages show a start-funnel CTA and an optional login action. They do not show quiz progress.
Intro pages show a CTA because they are copy-only transition screens and the user must intentionally continue.
Single choice pages do not show a CTA by default. Selecting an option saves the answer and advances to the next page.
Multi choice pages show a CTA because users may select several options before confirming.

Multi choice CTAs must stay disabled until the user selects at least minSelections options. Default minSelections is 1 unless the page explicitly sets allowEmptySelection: true for a deliberate skip-style question.
Input pages show a CTA because users need to confirm typed, selected, or adjusted values.
Account and subscription pages use explicit actions for viewing details, cancelling, resuming, returning to the funnel, or logging out.
